title: CAU Imphal ORDINANCE 7 of 1994
text: GovE:RNMENTOr INDIA MINISTRY OF AGRICULTURE DEPARTMENT OF AGRICULTURAL RESEARCH AND EDUCATION Central Agricultural University, Imphal (Manipur) ORDINANCE- Imphal, the 7th November, 1994 No 7 of 1994 No. VC CAU/14(ESTABLI))193: Whereas the Government of India.in the Department of Agricultural Research arid Education (Indian Council of Agricultural Research hS approved the making of the first Ordinance regarding the award of Junior Fellow:hips and Gold Medals to the students of the Central Agricultui al University. Imphal vide letter No. F.N0.42-1794-Edn. IV. dated the 25th October, 1994. Now. therefore. in ...:)Iacise of the powers conferred by sub-section (2) read with clause tit of sub-section of section 27 of the Central Agricultural University Act. 1992 (40 of 1992). and with the previous 'app., mil of the Central .Government. as atbresajd, I. the Vice-Chancellor-of the Central Agricultural University, Imphal. do hereby make the first Ordinance and notify the conditions and procedure for the award of the University Junior Fellowships and Gold Medal(s) to the students' of the Central Agricultural Univessity. Imphal. JUNIOR FELLOWSHIP (POST-GRADUATE STUDIES) I. There shall he two Central Agricultural 'University Fellowships for students undertaking Master programmes/studies in this University (hereinafter referred to as Junior Fellowships) of the value of' Rs. 600/- (six hundred) per month tach. tenable initially for 6 months (one semester), butextendable up to 2 years (4 semest rs). PfiejM -- 1 of 4 -- (c) The Director of Instruction shall prepare the merit list Of the studehtS seeking the Junior Fellowships. The list shall then be submitted to the. Vice-Chancellor for approval. As soon as the approval of the Vice-Chancellor is obtained, the Registrar shall notify the same to all concerned. (7) The Junior Fellowship shall be payable from the date of registration in the first semester of the Master's Degree Programme for which batch the award is made. (8) The Junior Fellowship shall not be payable for the semester when the holder remains absent ;:•id/or when the University teaching remains suspended-or for the period the semester is declared as "Zero Credit Semester". • (9) In case two candidates with equal OGPAirnarks in the Bachelor's Degree are ' admitted to the first semester, but shall be announced after determination of the merit in the subsequent semester(s) when their tie is removed. In such a ease, the Junior Fellowship shall be awarded from the date of admission to the said Programme. I 10)A recipient of the Junior Fellowship shall not be entitled to- receive any other type of financial assistance, except a loan scholarship from Central State Govemtnent(s) or ails' other organisation. CENTRAL AGRICULTURAL UNIVERSITY GOLD MEDALS Central Agricultural University Gold Medals, (hereinafter referred to as "University Gold Medals") shall he awarded to students of each Bachelor Degree Programme of the University in the manner and on the conditions prescribed below: 1. One University Gold Medal shall he awarded to a student of each Bachelor's Degree Programme who secures the first positiOn among the successful candidates with Overall Grade Point Average not leSs than 3.75 in 4.00 point scale in the said Degree Programme. . 2. In order to be eligible for the award, a graduating student should have completed graduation requirements consecutively within the minimum period of time prescribed for -- 2 of 4 -- ,at i,:peattag any examthationteourse and,lor obtaining' F- grade in any course ancror he irnpro ing grade many course 3.:\ student who had been on rA endemic and or conduct probation at anv time during the residence in the Central Agricultural University shall not he eligible for the award of the University Gold Medal. 4. If more than one student completing the gradiration in a particular programme secure the same OGPA, the consistency in the performance over their entire period of study. participation in the extra curricular activities as also age (younger preferred) shall he taken into consideration for the award of the University Gold Medal. The Vice- Chancellor shall in that case be advised in taking a decision by a Committee consisting of Director of Instruction, Dean of the concerned College and the Registrar. 5. The name of the insignia of the University shall be inscribed on one side of the medal and the name of the student with the degree and the year of passing on the reverse. Every such medal shall he accompanied with a Certificate of Merit bearing the signatures of the Registrar. Director of' Instruction and the Vice-Chancellor of the University: 6. The Gold Medals shall be awarded to the students at a regular University convocation in \vhich the successful candidates are to be awarded their degree certificates. In case any selected student(s) pray(s) for the award of the degree in absentia, the medal shall be handed over by the Vice-Chancellor after the regular University. convocation of the said batch. 7. The names of the Gold Medalists shall be displayed on an Honour's Board to be put up in the main building of the College concerned. (M.P. Singh).
